procurement.item: IRIS-T medium-range air defence system (jointly procured with Estonia)
ASCOD (Hunter) infantry fighting vehicles - first batch of 84 ordered
procurement.supplier: Santa Barbara Sistemas / General Dynamics European Land Systems
procurement.delivery: First vehicles spring 2026, entering service by autumn 2026
Gradual integration into regular Armed Forces units

procurement.value: Not disclosed; financed via EU Security Action Fund

news: Latvia signed a EUR 600 million contract with Diehl Defence (Germany) for IRIS-T medium-range air defence systems, jointly procured with Estonia, with delivery in 2026 - the largest military procurement since Latvia regained independence (2026-02-12).
Latvia is set to receive the first of 84 ordered ASCOD (Hunter) infantry fighting vehicles in spring 2026, with assembly at the Patria/GDELS plant in Trubia, Spain (2026-02-01).

Latvia's Ministry of Defence signed contracts with three Latvian firms (Brasa Defence Systems, Natrix, LV-Teh) for domestically-built unmanned ground systems, the first such procurement of locally developed ground drones (2026-04-10).
Latvia's Cabinet approved the first contract under a multi-year BLAZE interceptor drone framework with Riga-based Origin Robotics, financed through the EU Security Action Fund (2026-04-21).
Latvia's 2026 defence budget reached EUR 2.16 billion (4.91% of GDP), with continued work on centralising and streamlining procurement (2025-12-04).

upcoming_major_purchase.quantity: System quantity not publicly disclosed; jointly procured with Estonia
upcoming_major_purchase.notes: Described as the largest Latvian military procurement since independence; part of a wider 'echeloned air defence' build-up alongside Estonia.

nato_presence.notes: Canada leads the brigade under Operation REASSURANCE, its largest overseas military deployment; troop numbers fluctuate with rotations.
